# NNNN — <decision title: what was decided, not the topic>

**Implemented by:** _pending_
<!-- A single line, always present. When an implementing plan exists, replace
     `_pending_` with a link to it AND the implementing commits (grouped by
     task id), e.g.
       **Implemented by:** [docs/plans/active/<slug>.md](../plans/active/<slug>.md), commits abc1234 (T1) · def5678 (T2)
     then repoint the link to ../plans/archive/<slug>.md in the same commit that
     archives the plan. The commits mirror the plan's `commit refs:` frontmatter;
     all other as-built detail lives in the PLAN, never here. -->

<One-paragraph statement of the decision, in the present tense — what is now
true for the repo.>

## Status

<Human-readable lifecycle note; the frontmatter `status:` is authoritative.
Record when it was grilled/decided. If `rejected`, state the reason so the
option is not re-litigated. If `superseded by ADR-NNNN`, one line saying so —
and the superseding ADR carries `**Supersedes:** ADR-NNNN` under its title.>

## Context

<The forces, constraints, and prior decisions that shape this. The evidence —
this is where research, surveys, and prior-art belong.>

## Considered options

<The options weighed, each with its tradeoff; mark the chosen one. Use this
section when the choice is non-obvious and the rejected branches are worth
recording. Drop it (use the minimal template) when the decision is direct.>

## Decision

<The decision and its rationale. **This body is immutable once the frontmatter
`status:` is `accepted`** — a change is a NEW ADR that supersedes this one.
Never edit an accepted decision in place; never append dated amendments (that
append-only journal is the anti-pattern this standard retires).>

## Consequences

<What becomes true, what is now harder, what is explicitly deferred. As-built
implementation reality belongs in the implementing plan, not here.>
